upstream_meaning: 'shown' by an LLM shopping agent — possibly stripped, truncated, reordered, buried
downstream_meaning: a faithful rendering seen by a human — the atomic unit incrementality assumes

synthetic_inputs.name: agent_share
synthetic_inputs.label: [SYNTHETIC] corpus parameter. No public number exists for this; it is seeded only so the mechanism is bit-for-bit replayable — labelled exactly like the engine's '~18% omit cc_bin' synthetic. No Rokt-internal number is asserted.

synthetic_inputs.name: degraded_fraction
synthetic_inputs.label: [SYNTHETIC] corpus parameter. No public number exists for this; it is seeded only so the mechanism is bit-for-bit replayable — labelled exactly like the engine's '~18% omit cc_bin' synthetic. No Rokt-internal number is asserted.

grounding: Agent-mediated checkout and its near-zero sales lesson are public (research 26 #2/#3); everything else here is a modelling claim. The ACL refuses to count a degraded rendering as the measurement atom — it does not estimate anything, and no degradation rate is asserted: both fractions are labelled synthetic inputs.
note: Of 200 'impressions', 62 are a different unit (agent-degraded renderings). The Conformist path counts 200; the ACL counts 138 and refuses 62 — visibly, without estimating anything. The channel and degradation fractions are [SYNTHETIC]; the unit mismatch is the claim, not a rate.

illegal.message: cannot add recorded + incremental: the two counts belong to different bounded contexts and mean different things. Translate across the seam first (acl_translate), or the sum is a lie.
